examples: The mantis focused too much on its prey and neglected a bird behind staring covetously at it.
Furthermore, she never used her position to indulge in extravagances or covetously harm the emperor's concubines.
In the modern time, the imperialist powers eyed China covetously. Once the time was ripe, they began to carve up China.
Cash-strapped states look covetously at Indian casinos, which pay fewer taxes than businesses off the reservation.
When the Hummer H2 debuted its monstrous frame, my Dad covetously pointed out every single one on the road, especially the obnoxious yellow ones.
